texto: What's going on guys Tyler here on this channel we help you guys become better bass anglers and so today We're going to talk about the suspending jerkbait how to best fish it and of course catch some big ones. Let's talk about it So the suspending jerkbait is great at two things one suspending and two imitating a bait fish And of course as we all know bass love to eat bait fish. Now the cool thing about this like I said is it Suspends. So the first thing you got to do is make a long cast just like that 10 pound fluorocarbon is my favorite line for this technique and once you make that long cast You want to reel down with your rod tip I'd say five or six times to get that bait to dive down to its maximum depth And you can either jerk the rod tip up or down depending on how deep you want that jerkbait to go But one thing you want to make sure that you are doing is returning your rod tip to the exact same place To where you jerked it from. It's important to have a little bit of loose line on your retrieve Otherwise it's not a jerkbait. It's going to be kind of a pullbait If y'all want to see the full instructional video and tons of fish catches click the link below We'll see you guys next time. That's a thick fish Good creep

segmentos.text: What's going on guys Tyler here on this channel we help you guys become better bass anglers and so today
We're going to talk about the suspending jerkbait how to best fish it and of course catch some big ones. Let's talk about it
So the suspending jerkbait is great at two things one suspending and two imitating a bait fish
And of course as we all know bass love to eat bait fish. Now the cool thing about this like I said is it
Suspends. So the first thing you got to do is make a long cast just like that
10 pound fluorocarbon is my favorite line for this technique and once you make that long cast
You want to reel down with your rod tip I'd say five or six times to get that bait to dive down to its maximum depth
And you can either jerk the rod tip up or down depending on how deep you want that jerkbait to go
But one thing you want to make sure that you are doing is returning your rod tip to the exact same place
To where you jerked it from. It's important to have a little bit of loose line on your retrieve
Otherwise it's not a jerkbait. It's going to be kind of a pullbait
If y'all want to see the full instructional video and tons of fish catches click the link below
We'll see you guys next time. That's a thick fish
